name: Tate Press (Dumbbell Elbows-Out Extension)
resistance_profile: Lying with the elbows flared out, the dumbbells travel down to the chest and back up, loading the triceps hardest through the mid-range. Often used to bias the medial/lateral heads and build lockout strength.
cues: Point the elbows out and keep the upper arms vertical
Slide the dumbbells down the chest, not toward the face
execution_steps: Lie on a flat bench holding two dumbbells over the chest, palms facing your feet, arms straight.
Flare the elbows out and lower the dumbbells by bending the elbows so the plates travel down toward the upper chest.
Keep the upper arms roughly vertical as the dumbbells descend along the chest.
Extend the elbows to press the dumbbells back to straight arms, squeezing the triceps at lockout.
good_when: You want a lockout-strength and medial/lateral-head builder
Skullcrushers bother your elbows and you want an alternative path
Adding variety to dumbbell triceps work
bad_when: The elbows-out position aggravates your elbow joints
You are new to lying triceps work and lack control
common_errors: Letting the upper arms drift and turning it into a press
Lowering too fast and losing control near the chest
Using too heavy a load and compromising the path
progressions: Add a pause at the bottom
regressions: Switch to a cable pushdown or pressdown
